title: [FSMToHW] Add FSM to HW conversion pass
body: This commit introduces an FSM to HW conversion pass, as well as some small modifications to the FSM dialect to facilitate the conversion. The implementation diverges from #1506 in that: - strictly the HW/Seq dialects are targeted (with a few `sv.wire`s) with the goal of lowering the FSM to a structural RTL representation (as opposed to a behavioral style, with `sv` case statements). - Whereas #1506 allowed for software-like operations to be used within the various regions, this lowering strictly expects hardware-like operations. Conversion approach: - A state register is created of size `ceillog2(N states)` - Each state is encoded using an integer [0:N[ - For each state, transition, operations within their respective regions are copied into the `hw.module` - A states' next-state transition is a chain of 2:1 muxes perfoming the priority-encoded transition logic, with the final option being a transition to itself. - For each output of the FSM, a mux is used to select between the output of the states, selected by the current state register. - Similarly, a mux is used to select the next state based on the current state. e.g.:
